verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 (BiP) is an ER-luminal chaperone lacking a TM domain or GPI anchor. Baseline localization is ER-lumen only. However, it has extensively documented non-baseline surface display on cancer cells (where it functions as a ligand for immune receptors and growth-factor-pathway co-receptors) and during cell death (apoptosis, necroptosis) where it serves as a DAMP / 'eat me' signal. This qualifies as cell_state_induced surface accessibility.
